attributes.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the issued external-access JWT. Verification compares against the presented token's hash so individual tokens can be revoked (or replaced by minting a new one) without rotating the global secret.
When the external-access magic-link token was minted (typically at suggestion accept-time).
Hard expiry of the external-access token. Default policy: review due date + 4 weeks grace.
Manual or automatic revocation flag. Auto-revoked when the suggestion is declined or withdrawn, or when a replacement token is minted.
First time the reviewer clicked the magic link and successfully reached the landing page. Distinct from wmkf_emailopenedat (email-pixel open) — this is intent-to-engage.
Path to the reviewer's per-suggestion folder under the akoya_request library. Format: '{requestNumber}_{guidNoHyphensUpper}/Reviews/{suggestionGuid}'. Library is always 'akoya_request'.
True if foundation staff uploaded the review on the reviewer's behalf (paper, email-attachment, lost-link). False or null if reviewer self-served via the magic link.
The reviewer's title and organization at review-submission time, as entered on the form. Pre-filled from CRM if known but reviewer-editable, since affiliations shift between invite and submission.
Q1 of the review form: 'If the proposed project is successful in its entirety, how will it impact the field?' 99 is a sentinel for 'unable to answer' — filter (< 99) when computing aggregates.

options.label: Will result in publications of disciplinary interest
Will result in publications of broad interest

attributes.description: Q3 of the review form: 'How risky is the project overall?' Note: higher is NOT worse — Keck Foundation explicitly funds risky projects. Do not treat this as a quality signal in aggregate analysis. 99 is a sentinel for 'unable to answer'.

options.label: Low risk (will likely work in its entirety)
Medium risk (parts may succeed, others may fail)

attributes.description: Q10 of the review form: 'Please assign an overall rating to the proposal.' Higher = better, suitable for averaging. 99 is a sentinel for 'unable to answer' — filter (< 99) when computing aggregates.
